vulnerability.reference: Over-reliance on automated image generation without human quality verification in high-stakes domains like medical imaging.
vulnerability.generated: Reliance on skip-connection integrity without built-in error verification leaves users unable to detect or compensate for tuning-induced numerical distortion.

impact.reference: Misdiagnosis in medical contexts when degraded images are used for clinical decision support.
impact.generated: Generated images lose visual fidelity, misleading downstream decisions and eroding trust in diffusion-based synthesis.

dose_response.reference: Skip-tuning introduces numerical propagation errors that degrade denoising, which in training-free sampling distorts generated images, leading to misdiagnosis and eroded trust when flawed outputs are used without verification.
dose_response.generated: Skip-tuning numerical propagation errors in UNet diffusion models, through user reliance on high-fidelity image synthesis, triggers undetected distortion due to missing error verification, leading to lost visual fidelity, misled decisions, and eroded trust.
